description_long: This object is a rectangular, box-shaped electronic device with a matte black finish. The front panel features multiple rotary dials, toggle switches, and small indicator lights, as well as a horizontal analog meter, suggesting it is a control or measurement instrument. The sides and back are plain, with only a few protruding knobs or connectors visible, indicating minimal external interfacing. The device is constructed primarily from metal, likely steel or aluminum, with plastic or rubber components for the dials and switches. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed screws at the corners of the front panel for easy access or maintenance. Its size and configuration suggest it is intended for stationary use in an urban environment, possibly as part of a utility or monitoring system.
description: A matte black, box-shaped electronic control device with multiple dials, switches, and an analog meter on the front panel. The sides and back are plain, emphasizing its utilitarian design.
description_view_0: A plain, featureless side of the black box, with only a small knob or connector visible near the back.
description_view_1: The front panel displays several rotary dials, toggle switches, indicator lights, and an analog meter, all secured with visible screws.
description_view_2: Another plain side of the box, with a small protruding knob or connector, otherwise unadorned.
description_view_3: The back of the device is completely featureless, with a smooth matte surface and no visible connectors or openings.
attribute_control_interface: rotary dial, toggle switch, physical button
